---
title: Resurrection
description: Kill the app, open a different client with a different model, connect your wallet, and your full notes and canvas layout come back.
---

Resurrection is the test that proves the custody story is real. If the data truly outlives the app, then a brand-new client should be able to wake your full memory with nothing but your wallet. It can.

## What comes back

Open a different client, with a different model behind it, and connect the same wallet. From there:

1. The client discovers your vault from the chain (it scans for the vault your wallet created).
2. You approve one pairing transaction so this new client is an allowlisted agent that can decrypt.
3. It pulls your quilts from Walrus and rebuilds, decrypting as it goes. You see it work through them, quilt by quilt.

When it finishes, your notes are all there. So is your canvas layout: the arrangement of note cards on the board is itself stored as a durable note, so the constellation comes back intact too. Nothing about this depends on Anima being alive. It depends only on the chain and your wallet.

<Callout type="info">
"Connect your wallet" is not zero-signature. A fresh client has to register itself as an agent before it can decrypt, which is one wallet pairing transaction. After that one approval, the rebuild runs on its own.
</Callout>

## What does not come back

Resurrection restores what was meant to persist, and only that. Two things are ephemeral by design:

- **Live presence** (cursors, who is on the board, "X is writing") is never durable. It transits the relay and is forgotten, so there is nothing to resurrect. The same is true of the live edits in a shared session: they pass through the relay live, and only the sealed snapshot an allowlisted writer persists comes back.
- **Chat transcripts** do not persist. Only the notes distilled from a conversation are written to your vault. The raw back-and-forth is gone; the distilled memory is what survives.

This is not a limitation so much as the point. The durable layer (your notes and your layout) is wallet-owned and resurrectable. The transient layer (presence and chat) is deliberately left to evaporate.
